title: Chondrocyte Isolation from Human Cartilage
author: Sarah Snelling
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8uphwvn

steps.instruction: Make up Collagenase solution (1mg/ml) in standard medium, allow to dissolve and sterile filter through a 0.45um followed by 0.2um syringe filter. The 0.45um filter step is essential to enable efficient filtration. Collagenase mixture can be used fresh or immediately frozen and used up to 6 months after preparation. Store in aliquots as there is loss of activity with freeze-thaw cycles.
Wash cartilage-bone cut thoroughly in PBS by shaking for 1 minute.
Place cartilage-bone cut on a sterile cutting board and use a clean scalpel to scrape off any remaining blood or non-cartilage tissue from the cartilage surface
Cutting flat along the bone, remove full thickness pieces of cartilage from the bone surface and place in standard medium in a petri dish
Cut cartilage pieces (in media) in to approx 3mmx3mmxfull thickness pieces using a scalpel.
Place cartilage pieces in to collagenase digestion mix (10% v/v cartilage:digestion mix) in a 50ml tube
Incubate cartilage-collagenase mix for 12-15 hours at 37C with shaking (45-60rpm).
After digestion, pass mixture through a 70um cell sieve
Centrifuge filtered solution for 5 minutes at 300xg
Wash pellet, resuspend and repeat centrifugation step
Resuspend cells in 1ml standard medium until pellet is completely resuspended. Take volume up to 10ml and mix thoroughly by pipetting.
Count cells and adjust volume to 4x10 4 cells/ml in standard medium
Plate 10ml cell:media mixture into relevant number of 10cm cell-culture treated petri dishes and leave for 3-7 days to adhere at 37C in a CO 2 incubator
Once cells have adhered (this can take 3-7 days) refresh media and continue to refresh media every 2-3 days until cells reach 80% confluence.
Freeze: By washing cells in standard medium, scraping in approx 5ml per plate, and centrifuging in 15ml tubes before resuspending in ice cold 10%DMSO in FBS prior to immediate freezing.

OR: Passage cells by washing cells in standard medium, scraping in approx 5ml per plate, and centrifuging in 15ml tubes before resuspending in appropriate volume of medium for a 1:2 split (ie each 10cm dish becomes 2x10cm dishes). Cells should ideally be used at this passage.
